finding: Silent currency pair swap on reinvestment. When strike price is NOT met, the reinvestment screen swaps the currency pair (e.g., USD/SGD becomes SGD/EUR) without any banner, warning, or explanation.
novice_impact: Will not notice the swap. Believes they are continuing their original investment when entering a fundamentally different trade with different currency risk.
seasoned_impact: Will likely notice but finds the silent swap presumptuous. May have wanted to convert back to original base currency instead.
recommendation: Add a prominent banner: 'Your settlement was in [SGD]. Your new investment will use [SGD] as the base currency. This differs from your original pair [USD/SGD].' Offer choice between same pair or settlement-based pair.

finding: Scenario B does not show base-currency equivalent value. User sees '258,392.17 SGD' but cannot assess whether this represents a gain or loss relative to their 200,000 USD investment without mental FX arithmetic.
novice_impact: May interpret the large SGD number as a gain when it could represent a loss in USD terms. Fundamental informed consent failure.
seasoned_impact: Can estimate mentally but shouldn't have to. The absence looks like the product is hiding the downside.
recommendation: Add a line below Scenario B settlement: 'Equivalent to approximately ~USD [X] at current spot rate.' Flag in red if the equivalent is below original investment.

finding: Green/red color coding on scenario badges biases perception. 'Base Currency' badge is green (positive connotation), 'Alternate Currency' badge is red/coral (negative connotation). Both scenarios produce positive yield; the color coding creates an unfair implicit hierarchy.
novice_impact: Unconsciously interprets green as 'good outcome' and red as 'bad outcome,' potentially misunderstanding the product's actual risk profile.
seasoned_impact: Not misled but may note the design as potentially non-compliant with fair disclosure principles.
recommendation: Use neutral colors for both scenario badges (e.g., both navy/dark grey). Reserve green/red exclusively for gain/loss indicators in settlement breakdowns.

finding: No explicit risk statement or acknowledgment before Submit. The only pre-commitment safeguard is a single-line T&C link. No standalone risk disclosure, no plain-language summary of 'what could happen,' no friction step like a checkbox.
novice_impact: May submit without understanding they could receive a different currency. Highest-risk moment in the flow for user harm.
seasoned_impact: Would appreciate explicitness as regulatory protection.
recommendation: Add a risk summary box above Submit: 'Important: If the strike price is not met, your principal will be converted to [alternate currency] at the strike rate.' Require checkbox: 'I understand my investment may be settled in [alternate currency].'

finding: No error states, validation feedback, or loading states designed across any screen. No inline field validation errors, submission failures, network timeout states, processing indicators, or session expiry warnings.
novice_impact: Encountering an error provides no feedback about what went wrong. Could lead to duplicate submissions or abandoned transactions.
seasoned_impact: Expects enterprise-grade reliability indicators. Absence of loading states during submission is anxiety-inducing when real money is at stake.
recommendation: Design inline validation (min/max amount, numeric format), processing overlay for submission, error states with retry, session timeout warning, insufficient funds error.

finding: Strike price presented without any plain-language definition or explanation. Five radio options show values, '% from spot,' and 'Indicative yield' but never explain what strike price is, what 'from spot' means, or why higher strike reduces yield.
novice_impact: Cannot make an informed selection. This is the most consequential decision in the flow, presented without explanation.
seasoned_impact: Finds the compact layout efficient. A tooltip (not inline) would avoid cluttering their experience.
recommendation: Add contextual tooltip: 'The strike price determines the exchange rate threshold. If the market rate is above/below this at expiry, you receive base/alternate currency. Further from spot = higher yield but higher conversion probability.'

finding: Yield visually dominates risk in the strike price selector. Bold red yield percentages (right-aligned) draw the eye first; risk parameters ('% from spot') are in smaller grey text (left-aligned). Visual hierarchy inverts appropriate weighting for a risk-bearing product.
novice_impact: Focuses on yield (visually dominant) and may select highest yield without understanding the increased conversion probability.
seasoned_impact: Reads both columns but would appreciate equal visual weight and conversion probability language.
recommendation: Give 'from spot' percentage equal visual weight (same font size and weight). Consider reframing as 'conversion probability: higher/lower' to make risk tangible.

finding: Green overloaded with 5+ distinct meanings: success checkmark, 'Base Currency' badge, 'Strike Price Met' banner, positive yield amount, 'Active' status, and OCBC logo. Creates ambiguity between 'good for you' and 'base currency.'
novice_impact: Cannot distinguish semantic meanings of green across different contexts. May misinterpret 'Active' status as 'performing well.'
seasoned_impact: Can distinguish meanings from context but notes the inconsistency.
recommendation: Reserve green for explicit gain/positive indicators only. Use navy or brand colors for currency badges and neutral blue for informational status badges.

finding: Yield shown as annualized rate ('6.50% p.a.') without period return. For a 1-month tenor, actual return is ~0.54% (~USD 1,083 on USD 200,000). Users may expect 6.5% total return.
novice_impact: Dramatically overestimates return. May make investment decisions based on inflated expectations.
seasoned_impact: Understands annualization but still benefits from seeing period return for quick mental math.
recommendation: Show both: '6.50% p.a. (~0.54% for 1 month)' on strike price selectors. In Potential Outcomes, add: 'Estimated return: USD 1,083.33 (0.54% of principal).'

finding: Action-critical settlement notifications buried as inline banners within dense table. Strike Price Met/Not Met banners appear between table rows, competing visually with dense data. Most time-sensitive items are not visually prioritized.
novice_impact: May miss critical settlement notifications that require reinvestment decisions.
seasoned_impact: With many investments, must scan the full table to identify which need action. Inefficient.
recommendation: Create a 'Requires Action' card section at the top of the Active Investments view with prominent Reinvest/View Details CTAs. Keep the full table below for monitoring.

finding: Warning triangle icon on 'Strike Price Not Met' implies error/problem when it is a normal, expected product outcome. For users who placed DCI for currency conversion purposes, alternate-currency settlement may be the desired outcome.
novice_impact: Sees warning icon and panics: 'Did something go wrong?' Creates unnecessary anxiety about a normal product function.
seasoned_impact: Finds the warning treatment inaccurate or patronizing.
recommendation: Use a neutral blue information icon instead of warning triangle. Change banner to outcome-neutral: 'Your investment will be settled in [alternate currency] at the strike rate.'

finding: Account gate uses completely different nav bar configuration ('FX and treasury' lowercase, 'Trade finance' active) and is a dead end with no timeline, alternatives, or back navigation. Different nav config from all other DCI screens.
novice_impact: Believes they navigated to the wrong section. Combined with unfamiliar account requirement, high bounce probability.
seasoned_impact: Confused by nav label discrepancy. Frustrated by lack of timeline information for account opening.
recommendation: Use same nav bar config as rest of DCI flow. Add estimated processing time, required documents, 'Learn More' link, and back navigation.

finding: '$0,000.00 USD' minimum amount is a formatting bug/template error. In a financial product, displaying a malformed monetary value undermines trust at the very first input field.
novice_impact: May interpret as 'no minimum' or be confused. Trust in platform accuracy damaged immediately.
seasoned_impact: Recognizes as a bug and questions reliability of other displayed values (rates, yields, amounts).
recommendation: Fix data binding to display actual minimum (e.g., 'Minimum amount: 50,000.00 USD'). Ensure currency format consistency.

finding: Navigation label inconsistency: 'FX and Investment' (most screens) vs 'FX and Treasury' (details screens) vs 'FX and treasury' (MCA gate). Three different nav bar configurations within the same product flow.
novice_impact: May believe they accidentally left the DCI section. Hesitates to proceed.
seasoned_impact: Notices inconsistency as unprofessional but not blocked.
recommendation: Standardize to 'FX and Investment' across all DCI screens. Audit all screens against a single nav component.

finding: No step indicator or progress bar across the multi-step placement flow. User moves through Parameters > Account Selection > Review > Confirmation with no visual indication of position or steps remaining.
novice_impact: Has no mental model of journey length. 'How many more screens until I commit?' is unanswered, increasing anxiety.
seasoned_impact: Can infer the flow but cannot confirm they are on the penultimate step before submission.
recommendation: Add horizontal step indicator: 'Step 1 of 3: Investment Parameters > Step 2 of 3: Account Selection > Step 3 of 3: Review & Submit.'

finding: Confirmation is a dead end. Only CTA is 'Place Another Investment.' No link to view the investment in portfolio, view investment details, or return to DCI landing page.
novice_impact: Wants to verify investment is 'in the system.' Without a direct link, feels uncertain and navigates around trying to find it.
seasoned_impact: Forced to navigate manually — a 2-3 click tax per transaction.
recommendation: Add 'View Investment Details' (primary) and 'Return to DCI Portfolio' (link) alongside existing 'Place Another Investment.'

finding: Scenario condition language differs between screens. Parameters: 'If fixing rate is >= 1.2850 on expiry.' Review: 'If spot rate >= 1.2850 at maturity.' Different rate names and time references for the same condition.
novice_impact: May believe these are different conditions, creating confusion about when and how outcome is determined.
seasoned_impact: Recognizes they are the same but finds inconsistency unprofessional.
recommendation: Standardize: 'If the market exchange rate is at or above [strike price] on the Expiry Date ([date])' across all screens.

finding: Collapsed confirmation lacks key parameter summary. User sees success message and reference number but must expand to verify investment details (amount, currency pair, strike, yield).
novice_impact: Needs immediate reassurance correct investment was placed. Having to expand creates unnecessary anxiety.
seasoned_impact: Placing multiple investments, wants to quickly confirm parameters without expanding.
recommendation: Show brief summary above timeline: 'USD 200,000.00 | USD/SGD | Strike: 1.2850 | Yield: 6.50% p.a. | Maturity: 19 Dec 2025.'

finding: Cross-currency KPI aggregation unclear. 'Total Invested Amount: 1,500,000.00 SGD' aggregates USD, EUR, and AUD investments into SGD equivalent without disclosing the conversion basis.
novice_impact: May not realize the total is a cross-currency aggregate and take at face value.
seasoned_impact: Questions the accuracy of cross-currency aggregation.
recommendation: Label as 'Total Invested (SGD equivalent)' or show separate totals per base currency.

finding: Potential Outcomes visual treatment changes between Parameters and Review screens. Parameters uses white cards with colored badges; Review uses full-width cards with colored backgrounds. Layout shifts from two-column to single-column.
novice_impact: May not recognize these as the same scenarios from the previous screen.
seasoned_impact: Adjusts but notices the inconsistency.
recommendation: Maintain consistent visual treatment for Potential Outcomes cards across all screens.

finding: Status badges use inconsistent visual treatments: 'Active' is text-only, 'Expiring Soon' is orange text, 'Awaiting Settlement' is green outline pill. No unified badge component.
novice_impact: Inconsistent badge styling reduces scannability when monitoring multiple investments.
seasoned_impact: Notices component immaturity as sign of design system fragmentation.
recommendation: Standardize all status badges to use the same shape and size, varying only in color. All should be pill badges.

finding: 'Expiring Soon' status in table provides no urgency context — no expiry date inline, no countdown, no indication of required action.
novice_impact: Doesn't understand urgency or what action to take.
seasoned_impact: Must click into detail screen to find expiry date, forcing unnecessary navigation.
recommendation: Add expiry date inline or as tooltip: 'Expires 10 Nov 2025 (2 days).'

finding: Filter controls (All Currencies, Status, Tenor, Search) visible on empty state when there is no data to filter.
novice_impact: Minor confusion: 'Why are there filters if I have no investments?'
seasoned_impact: No impact.
recommendation: Hide filter controls when there are no investments.

finding: Landing page value propositions are generic and marketing-heavy. 'Maximise your investment returns' and 'Great for currency conversion needs' don't differentiate DCI from a deposit or FX forward.
novice_impact: Doesn't learn anything specific about DCI from these cards.
seasoned_impact: Ignores marketing content.
recommendation: Replace with specific value props: 'Earn yields of X-Y% p.a. on your idle foreign currency' and 'Choose your preferred conversion rate while earning returns.'
